items.title: Exhibition sheds fresh light on WA's pre-colonial history

children.content: Oral histories from Menang Noongar elders are combined with AI renderings of life before colonisation in an exhibition at the WA State Library.

gallery.altText: Woman with bright orange jacket stands in front of bushland

thumbnail.altText: Woman with bright orange jacket stands in front of bushland
Lady with short black hair and a nosering stands against blue indigenous style dot artwork.
A short haired woman with glasses and a grey jacket stands in front of a black and white print.

items.title: The scale of the mouse plague is hard to fathom. Here's a visualisation

children.content: A mouse plague is invading farms and homes in southern and Western Australia. But what does that really look like?

image.altText: A graphic showing a top-down view of a bedroom where several mice have invaded.

items.title: Psychiatrist repeatedly harassed nurse on messaging platforms

children.content: The only psychiatrist in a regional WA area is given a more lenient sanction for sexually harassing a nurse, partially due to a lack of other mental health specialists in the area.

image.altText: A close-up shot of a doctor noting down symptoms of a patient on a form with a stethoscope hanging around his neck.

items.title: Black mould and broken toilets: Inside 'dismal' government-owned homes

children.content: Bug infestations, walls covered in black mould and broken toilets are just some of the issues plaguing homes in Australia's most isolated communities, where the landlord is the state government.

gallery.altText: Black mould and insects cover a wall and ceiling around Christian statues.
A man walking towards a tin building with palm trees along the side.
A kitchen sink with broken cupboards underneath and a window above covered by dirty sheet

thumbnail.altText: Black mould and insects cover a wall and ceiling around Christian statues.
A man walking towards a tin building with palm trees along the side.
A kitchen sink with broken cupboards underneath and a window above covered by dirty sheet

items.title: WA health official wants more support to stem 'rare' diphtheria outbreak

children.content: The director of the Communicable Disease Control Directorate says vaccines help prevent more serious symptoms, but more efforts are needed to tackle the diphtheria outbreak in remote areas.

image.altText: A nurse preparing a boostrix vaccination, the vaccine's white and purple box is visible in the foreground.

items.title: Albany's hidden WWII fuel tanks transformed into global street art gallery

children.content: Tucked away in bushland on Western Australia's south coast, a hidden World War II site has become a sprawling canvas for street artists from all over the world.

image.altText: A woman looks at the tanks

items.title: 'No sign of danger': Friend details horror of fatal shark attack

children.content: The best friend of a Perth father killed by a shark off Rottnest Island says there was "absolutely no warning" before the attack.

gallery.altText: Steven Mattaboni smiling with on a boat while a young girl steers.
Liam Thomas at beach, wide shot.
Steven Mattaboni with his wife Shirene in formalwear.

thumbnail.altText: Steven Mattaboni smiling with on a boat while a young girl steers.
Liam Thomas at beach, wide shot.
Steven Mattaboni with his wife Shirene in formalwear.

items.title: Maldives cave dive deaths leave us with many theories and one big question

children.content: The question of why experienced divers would descend to depths of more than 50 metres on a single tank of air — let alone enter a cave while doing so — baffles members of the relatively close-knit deep-sea diving community.

image.altText: A deep-sea diver descends into a cave system, trailing a guide line.

items.title: Fifty years ago, a killer returned the knife to the kitchen and slipped away

children.content: Teenage Dawn Hillier was a "ray of sunshine". Almost 50 years later, years of investigation, a coronial inquiry, and a big reward have been unable to solve her brutal murder.

image.altText: A graphic showing murder victim Dawn Hillier

items.title: 'I've had enough': Why this loyal Labor voter is switching to One Nation

children.content: The next opportunity to test support for One Nation in Western Sydney will be the state election on March 13. Already, disillusioned Labor voter Silvana Rota is ready to "give them a go".

image.altText: A woman wearing glasses and a purple t shirt

items.title: What's going wrong inside the City of Perth?

children.content: It's been labelled "dysfunctional" and "diabolical". What does the lord mayor have to say about the embattled City of Perth?

gallery.altText: Bruce Reynolds gives a subtle, toothless smile to the camera in front of council buildings.
Michelle Reynolds stands looking serious in a corportate head shot.
An exterior shot of Perth Council House.

thumbnail.altText: Bruce Reynolds gives a subtle, toothless smile to the camera in front of council buildings.
Michelle Reynolds stands looking serious in a corportate head shot.
An exterior shot of Perth Council House.

items.title: Giants create AFL history with onslaught, Dees and Dogs locked in classic

children.content: Greater Western Sydney has left reigning premier Brisbane reeling with a record-breaking goal surge, while the Demons and Bulldogs are locked in a classic at Docklands. Follow live.

image.altText: Toby Greene celebrates a goal against the Lions

items.title: Heavyweight champ avoids shock upset with controversial stoppage

children.content: Ukraine's unbeaten heavyweight world champion Oleksandr Usyk stopped Dutch former kickboxer Rico Verhoeven with one second left in the penultimate round of their WBC title fight.

image.altText: Boxer relieved as ref stops fight, talking to defeated boxer

items.title: What is 'pink-slime' journalism and has it infiltrated Australian media?

children.content: Experts say a series of AI-generated digital news outlets is the first recorded example of "pink-slime" journalism in Australia, with the website owner claiming the now-deleted outlets were an experiment gone wrong.

gallery.altText: A hand holding a phone wiht the bunbury guardian home page displayed on the screen with a pink background ebhind the phone

thumbnail.altText: A hand holding a phone wiht the bunbury guardian home page displayed on the screen with a pink background ebhind the phone

items.title: Export name change coming for Australian prosecco

children.content: Australia is the only country outside Italy that can use the name prosecco, but its export labels will need to change in the coming years.

image.altText: A glass of white sparkling wine held up towards the sun

items.title: Five economists share opinions on Labor's budget and Coalition's reply

children.content: Five leading economists weigh in on the budget and reply debate, embracing many of the ideas raised while also arguing that "bigger tax reform is needed."

image.altText: A man in a suit stands at a podium in parliament.

items.title: 'An absolutely pleasant surprise': Why single mums are moving in together

children.content: As rental and living costs surge, and as single mums remain one of the most vulnerable groups in the country, some are moving in together and finding many benefits.

image.altText: Hayly Grimmond and Anja Homburg and their children smile while siting on a bed.

items.title: 'Bushwalkers have died attempting this track': Instagram post glosses over hike's dangers

children.content: It looks beautiful in the Instagram post with blue skies and picturesque tarns carved by glaciers. But read further and the multi-day Tasmanian hike is considered one of Australia's most challenging bushwalks. In fact, it can be deadly.

image.altText: Procyon Peak, Western Arthur Range Dan Broun

items.title: Premier's fracking warning over possible gas shortfall sparks outrage

children.content: Roger Cook raises the prospect of gas fracking in WA's Kimberley region, warning the state could face future energy shortfalls if Woodside Energy's massive Browse project does not proceed.

gallery.altText: Roger Cook speaks at a press conference while wearing a high vis vest.
A close-up shot of protesters outside Parliament House in Perth holding signs saying 'Save Scott Reef' and opposing fracking.
A crowd of protesters outside Parliament House in Perth holding signs saying 'Save Scott Reef' and opposing fracking.

thumbnail.altText: Roger Cook speaks at a press conference while wearing a high vis vest.
A close-up shot of protesters outside Parliament House in Perth holding signs saying 'Save Scott Reef' and opposing fracking.
A crowd of protesters outside Parliament House in Perth holding signs saying 'Save Scott Reef' and opposing fracking.
